title: EU Supervisory Authorities warn consumers of risks and limited protection for certain crypto-assets and providers
text:
Back to Overview

EU Supervisory Authorities warn consumers of risks and limited protection for certain crypto-assets and providers
EU Supervisory Authorities warn consumers of risks and limited protection for certain crypto-assets and providers
09.10.25
•
Supervision and Regulation
The European Supervisory Authorities (EBA, EIOPA and ESMA – the ESAs) issued a warning to consumers, reminding that crypto-assets can be risky and that legal protection, if any, may be limited depending on which crypto-assets they invest in.
This warning is accompanied by a factsheet explaining what the new EU regulation on Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) means for consumers. The ESAs recommend concrete steps consumers can take to make informed decisions before investing in crypto-assets, such as checking if the provider is authorised in the EU.

Was bedeutet MICA für Sie als Verbraucher?

Warnung der ESAs
Kryptowerte können riskant sein und Ihr Schutz, falls vorhanden, kann begrenzt sein
